LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BERNARD N. WOLF'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. SINCE DEFENDANT DID NOT REPRESENT PLAINTIFF DURING THE REMAND PROCEEDINGS, THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS RAN FROM THE DATE OF THE FIRST COURT OF APPEAL DECISION. ALTERNATIVELY, DEFENDANT'S REPRESENTATION WAS TERMINATED BY PLAINTIFF ON MARCH 20, 2006 AND THIS SUIT WAS NOT FILED UNTIL MARCH 27, 2007. THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DISGORGEMENT IS BASED UPON PLAINTIFF'S LEGAL MALPRACTICE CLAIM AND IS BARRED BY THE ONE YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS, C.C.P. SECTION 340.6. FURTHER, IT IS UNLIKELY THAT PLAINTIFF CAN STATE A CLAIM BASED UPON DEFENDANT'S PREPARATION OF THE STATEMENT OF DECISION AND THE ALLEGATIONS RELATED TO HANDLING THE APPEAL LACK THE REQUISITE SPECIFICITY. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BERNARD WOLF'S DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION ARE FOR LEGAL MALPRACTICE AND ARE GOVERNED BY CCP SECTION 340.6. PLAINTIFF'S SUIT WAS FILED ON MARCH 27, 2007 AND PLAINTIFF'S INJURY OCCURRED IN JUNE OF 2004. ABSENT TOLLING, THE CLAIM IS BARRED. SECTION 340.6 MAY BE TOLLED ONLY AS DELINEATED IN THE STATUTE ITSELF. IN THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, PLAINTIFF ALLEGED THAT AFTER THE MALPRACTICE OCCURRED THAT PLAINTIFF "RETAINED MADOW TO HANDLE THE BRIEFINGS IN SAID APPEAL." THE RECORD REFLECTS THAT IS SO AND THAT DEFENDANT HAD AN UNSPECIFIED BUT A REDUCED ROLE THEREAFTER. AS A CONSEQUENCE, IT APPEARS THAT THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS MAY NOT HAVE BEEN TOLLED BY THE CONTINUING REPRESENTATION RULE. FOXBOROUGH V. VAN ATTA (1994) 26 CAL.APP.4TH 217. HOWEVER, THE COURT NEED NOT REACH THAT POINT. IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT PLAINTIFF REPLACED DEFENDANT AS COUNSEL ON MARCH 20, 2006 AND THAT THIS COMPLAINT WAS NOT FILED UNTIL MARCH 27, 2007. PLAINTIFF'S RELIANCE ON CCP SECTION 351, AS ALLEGED IN PARAGRAPH 5 OF THE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT, IS WITHOUT MERIT GIVEN THE EXPRESS LANGUAGE OF CCP SECTION 340.6 AND THE HOLDING IN LAIRD V. BLACKER (1992) 2 CAL.4TH 606, 618. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
ORDER ON BERNARD WOLF'S DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT (SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND) (NO COPY, NO SASE)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BERNARD N. WOLF'S DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. IN THE COURT'S PRIOR ORDERS, THE COURT HAS SPELLED OUT WHY THE COMPLAINT IS BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS, CCP 340.6. THE 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT ADDS NO ADDITIONAL FACTS AND FOR THE REASONS STATED, PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM IS BARRED. PLAINTIFF'S 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION IS NOTHING MORE THAN A RESTATEMENT OF PLAINTIFF'S LEGAL MALPRACTICE CLAIM WITH A REFERENCE TO B & P CODE 17200. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO ALLEGE FACTS THAT SUPPORT SUCH A CLAIM. KHOURY V. MALY'S OF CALIFORNIA (1993) 14 CAL APP 4TH 612, 619. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Daniel Berko, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Richard L. Miller
Julian Pardo De Zela, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Bernard N. Wolf

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. Plaintiff Richard Miller's Motion To Compel Appearance Of Bernard N. Wolf At Deposition, Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions.
The Court previously having sustained demurrer without leave to amend, there are no issues pending on which to conduct discovery. Therefore, plaintiff's motion to compel appearance of Bernard N. Wolf at deposition and for production of documents, and plaintiff's request for sanctions are denied. Commissioner: Paul Slavit, not reported.

DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-24-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BERNARD N. WOLF'S DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT

Appearances:
Daniel Berko (771-6174) for plaintiff and responding party
Christian Foote of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney (788-1900) for defendant and moving party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. In the Court's prior orders, the Court has spelled out why the complaint is barred by the statute of limitations, CCP 340.6. The 4th amended complaint adds no additional facts and for the reasons stated, plaintiff's claim is barred. Plaintiff's 2nd cause of action is nothing more than a restatement of plaintiff's legal malpractice claim with a reference to B & P Code 17200. Plaintiff has failed to allege facts that support such a claim. Khoury v. Maly's of California (1993) 14 Cal App 4th 612, 619. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-27-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BERNARD WOLF DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT

DEFENDANT BERNARD WOLF'S DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: DANIEL BERKO, ESQUIRE (415.771.6174) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. CHRISTIAN PENN FOOTE, ESQUIRE (MURPHY PEARSON BRADLEY & FEENEY, 415.788.1900) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: sustained with TEN DAYS leave to amend. Both causes of action are for legal malpractice and are governed by CCP section 340.6. Plaintiff's suit was filed on March 27, 2007 and plaintiff's injury occurred in June of 2004. Absent tolling, the claim is barred. Section 340.6 may be tolled only as delineated in the statute itself. In the Second Amended Complaint, plaintiff alleged that after the malpractice occurred that plaintiff "retained Madow to handle the briefings in said appeal." The record reflects that is so and that defendant had an unspecified but a reduced role thereafter. As a consequence, it appears that the statute of limitations may not have been tolled by the continuing representation rule. Foxborough v. Van Atta (1994) 26 Cal.App.4th 217. However, the Court need not reach that point. It is undisputed that plaintiff replaced defendant as counsel on March 20, 2006 and that this complaint was not filed until March 27, 2007. Plaintiff's reliance on CCP section 351, as alleged in paragraph 5 of the Third Amended Complaint, is without merit given the express language of CCP section 340.6 and the holding in Laird v. Blacker (1992) 2 Cal.4th 606, 618. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/VC) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-24-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BERNARD N. WOLF'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: DANIEL BERKO, ESQUIRE (415.771.6174, EXT 215) APPEARING THROUGH COURTCALL ON BEHALF OF THE PLAINTIFF. CHRISTIAN FOOTE, ESQUIRE (MURPHY PEARSON BRADLEY & FEENEY, 415.788.1900) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. SINCE DEFENDANT DID NOT REPRESENT PLAINTIFF DURING THE REMAND PROCEEDINGS, THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS RAN FROM THE DATE OF THE FIRST COURT OF APPEAL DECISION. ALTERNATIVELY, DEFENDANT'S REPRESENTATION WAS TERMINATED BY PLAINTIFF ON MARCH 20, 2006 AND THIS SUIT WAS NOT FILED UNTIL MARCH 27, 2007. THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DISGORGEMENT IS BASED UPON PLAINTIFF'S LEGAL MALPRACTICE CLAIM AND IS BARRED BY THE ONE YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS, C.C.P. SECTION 340.6. FURTHER, IT IS UNLIKELY THAT PLAINTIFF CAN STATE A CLAIM BASED UPON DEFENDANT'S PREPARATION OF THE STATEMENT OF DECISION AND THE ALLEGATIONS RELATED TO HANDLING THE APPEAL LACK THE REQUISITE SPECIFICITY. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/VC) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
